short_blurb: Reliable roofing contractor serving Tarzana homes and businesses.
local_note: Tarzana's roofing mix includes 1950s–70s ranch homes, newer two-story builds, and commercial properties along Ventura Blvd. The area's hot dry summers accelerate shingle aging, and many Tarzana roofs show UV degradation, curling, and lost granules. We offer free inspections and photo documentation so homeowners can see exactly what's happening up top before committing to any work.

faqs.q: How often should I have my Tarzana roof inspected?
faqs.a: We recommend a professional inspection every 3–5 years, and after any major windstorm or rain event. Tarzana's Santa Ana wind exposure can lift flashing and crack older shingles — catching it early avoids interior water damage.
